description_long: This object is a tall, slender wildflower with three prominent white daisy-like blooms, each featuring a bright yellow central disc. The flowers are arranged at different heights along a thin, green stem that gently curves and branches. The petals are narrow, radiating symmetrically around the yellow centers, and appear slightly overlapping. Several jagged, lanceolate green leaves are attached to the stem at intervals, adding texture and contrast. The overall structure is delicate, with the flowers positioned at the ends of the stems, giving a natural, airy appearance. This type of flower is commonly found in urban parks, roadside verges, or garden beds.
description: A tall, delicate wildflower with three white daisy-like blooms and yellow centers on a slender green stem with jagged leaves.
description_view_0: Three white flowers with yellow centers are clearly visible, with the tallest bloom at the top and two others staggered below; the stem and leaves are fully exposed.
description_view_1: The flowers are seen more edge-on, with one bloom facing sideways and the others partially obscured; the stem's curvature is more pronounced.
description_view_2: The two main flowers face forward, showing their yellow centers and white petals, while the third flower is partially hidden behind the stem.
description_view_3: The flowers are angled away, with the petals and yellow centers visible from a slightly oblique perspective; the leaves and stem are more prominent.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, daisy-like configuration around the central disc, with each petal evenly spaced and slightly overlapping, forming a symmetrical whorl typical of the Asteraceae family.
